content:
I’m not a monster. I just want to be seen. Every night, I watch from the dark corners of the old house, where no one dares to come. They call me scary, but they don’t know my story.

Long ago, I was a little boy, just like you. I had no friends and felt very lonely. When I tried to play, others ran away, afraid of my twisted shadow and strange voice. So, I hid away, hoping someone might come to find me.

But the more I waited, the sadder I became. Now, I scare people so they won’t get close—because it hurts too much when they leave. If only someone would look past my scary face and hear my quiet wish: “Please don’t be afraid. I just want a friend.” Maybe then, I wouldn’t have to be alone in the dark anymore.
